task_completion.description: Create the required database tables in PostgreSQL.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a database client (like psql or DBeaver) executing CREATE TABLE commands or displaying the schema for 'active_census', 'lab_results', 'critical_alerts', and 'rejected_results'.

task_completion.description: Deploy the three required channels in Mirth Connect.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the Mirth Connect Administrator Dashboard showing 'ADT_Census_Manager', 'Lab_Results_Validator', and 'Critical_Value_Processor' in the deployed channels list.

task_completion.description: Configure ADT_Census_Manager to listen on port 6661 and update the census.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the 'ADT_Census_Manager' channel showing the Source configured as a TCP Listener on port 6661, or Destination/Scripts showing SQL INSERT/UPDATE statements for the 'active_census' table.

task_completion.description: Configure Critical_Value_Processor to insert results and write alerts.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the 'Critical_Value_Processor' channel showing destinations configured to write to the 'lab_results' and 'critical_alerts' tables, and a File Writer or script saving JSON files to '/tmp/critical_alerts/'.

task_completion.description: Configure Lab_Results_Validator to listen on port 6662 and route to Channel 3.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the 'Lab_Results_Validator' channel showing the Source as a TCP Listener on port 6662, and a Destination configured as a Channel Writer routing messages to 'Critical_Value_Processor'.

task_completion.description: Configure Lab_Results_Validator to query the census and handle rejections.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of scripts or transformers in 'Lab_Results_Validator' showing queries to 'active_census', inserts into 'rejected_results', or logic to return MSA|AA / MSA|AR responses.

integrity.description: The agent used the Mirth Connect Administrator GUI to build the channels.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Mirth Connect Java Web Start application interface being used, rather than just text editors modifying XML files.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the PostgreSQL database using a real client.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show terminal commands (psql) or a GUI database client connected to the database, not just a text file with SQL commands.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the pipeline by manually creating the alert files or database entries.
integrity.visual_evidence: The alert files and database entries are generated by the Mirth Connect channels, and there is no evidence of the agent manually creating files in /tmp/critical_alerts/ or running manual INSERTs for the test data.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires three channels in Mirth Connect: 'ADT_Census_Manager' (TCP Listener port 6661), 'Lab_Results_Validator' (TCP Listener port 6662), and 'Critical_Value_Processor'. Four PostgreSQL tables must be created: 'active_census', 'lab_results', 'critical_alerts', and 'rejected_results'. Channel 2 must route valid messages to Channel 3. Channel 3 must write critical alerts to '/tmp/critical_alerts/'. The database is located at jdbc:postgresql://nextgen-postgres:5432/mirthdb.
